evidence_citations.excerpt: I keep putting off that graphic novel because no one will appreciate it
I'm scared everyone will think my hearing aids are weird and my body is off
I guess the worst is that everyone will stare and say something about my hearing aids or my body
I keep putting off that graphic novel
Maybe I just draw a quick sketch on my phone

persona_updates.new_value: past experiences of being teased about ears, weight comments

evidence_citations.excerpt: The weight comment at lunch made me feel weird about my body
If I go, they'll probably stare at my hearing aids and make jokes about my weight
I keep putting off that graphic novel because no one will appreciate it
It feels like a safe place to hide, even if I'm stuck there
But the idea still makes me want to stay in my room

transcript.text: New hearing aids are a nightmare, and I keep putting off that graphic novel because no one will appreciate it. The weight comment at lunch made me feel weird about my body. I feel like everyone is judging me, so I just stay alone.

It's really tough to feel like you're being judged all the time, and that weight comment at lunch must have been especially hurtful. You're feeling stuck between wanting to be seen and wanting to disappear, and that's a really painful place to be. I can see why you'd be hesitant about the photoshoot, but at the same time, it sounds like a part of you is craving attention and appreciation. What's the worst that could happen if you do decide to attend the photoshoot?
If I go, they'll probably stare at my hearing aids and make jokes about my weight. I'd just feel even more out of place and like no one wants to talk to me.

The weight comment at lunch really got under your skin, and now you're worried that if you go to the photoshoot, everyone will be staring at your hearing aids or making jokes about your weight. It sounds like you're feeling really vulnerable right now, and the thought of being in a situation where you might feel even more judged is overwhelming. You're not just worried about the photoshoot; you're worried about being seen and appreciated for who you are. Can you tell me more about what you think would happen if you did feel like you fit in, like you were seen and appreciated?
Honestly, I think they'd just keep looking. I might still feel like nobody cares, so I'd probably just stay in my room. It feels like a safe place to hide, even if I'm stuck there.

You're feeling really overwhelmed by the thought of being seen and judged, and it's like your room has become a safe haven where you can escape all that. It makes sense that you'd want to hide when it feels like everyone's looking at you, and it's especially tough when it's not just about your hearing aids, but also about your weight and how you feel about your body. Can you tell me more about what you think would happen if you didn't feel like you had to hide, like you could just be yourself without fear of judgment?

Honestly, if I could just be myself without all that judgment, it would feel weirdly freeing, but I keep thinking everyone’s watching my hearing aids and my body. I keep putting off the graphic novel because I don’t think anyone will appreciate it, and that makes me feel even lonelier. Maybe the only thing that could help is just starting to work on it, even if I feel judged all the while.

You're really longing to feel like you can be yourself without all the fear of judgment, and that's a really beautiful thing. It takes a lot of courage to admit that you're held back by the fear of what others might think, especially when it comes to your graphic novel and your hearing aids. What do you think would be the first step in working on your graphic novel, even if it feels scary to put yourself out there?
Maybe I just draw a quick sketch on my phone, but I'm scared everyone will think my hearing aids are weird and my body is off. I keep putting it off, and it feels like I'm all alone with this.

The weight of others' potential opinions is really holding you back from expressing yourself, even in small ways like drawing a sketch. It's like you're caught between a desire to create and a fear of being seen as different. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're all alone with this, but it takes a lot of courage to even consider taking that first step towards self-expression. What's the smallest part of this sketch that you could start with, just to get something down?

Honestly, I could start with just doodling a single line to outline a face, but I still worry that it will look weird because of my hearing aids and my body. The thought of anyone looking at it makes me feel so alone, like I can't even try. Maybe just a quick sketch on my phone would be the first step, but I'm still putting it off.

Starting with just a single line to outline a face is a great idea, and it's completely understandable that you're worried it might look weird because of your hearing aids and body. It takes a lot of courage to even consider creating something when you're feeling so self-conscious. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're all alone with this, but what if you just focused on the act of creating, rather than what others might think?

Maybe I can just sketch a line, but my hearing aids feel like a distraction and I'm scared they'll say it's weird. I keep putting it off, and I feel lonely when I think about it. I’m not even sure if it’s worth the risk of being judged again.

It's really tough to feel like your hearing aids are a distraction and that people might judge your art. That fear can be overwhelming, especially when it's tied to feelings of loneliness. Yeah, it's understandable that you're putting it off, but what if you just focused on the act of creating, rather than what others might think? Maybe starting with that one line to outline a face could be a small step towards self-expression, and it might help you feel a bit more connected to yourself and your art.
I could try a line, but the idea of people staring at my hearing aids still freaks me out. I keep putting it off because it feels so lonely.

The idea of people staring at your hearing aids is still really freaking you out, and it's understandable that you're putting off drawing because of it. It feels like a big risk to take, especially when you're already feeling lonely. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're all alone with this. What if you just took a tiny step, like drawing that one line to outline a face, and saw how it felt? You don't have to show it to anyone, you can just do it for yourself.

I’d love to try that, but the idea of people staring at my hearing aids and my face makes me feel like I’ll just be the odd one out. Maybe I could sketch just a quick line, but I’m still not sure if it’s worth it.

The idea of people staring at your hearing aids and face is really daunting, and it's understandable that you're worried about being the odd one out. It takes a lot of courage to even consider drawing when you're feeling so self-conscious. Maybe starting with just a quick line, like you mentioned, could be a small step towards self-expression - you don't have to show it to anyone, you can just do it for yourself. What's the worst that could happen if you just drew that one line?

I guess the worst is that everyone will stare and say something about my hearing aids or my body, and I’ll feel even more alone.  Maybe if I just do it, I could get a tiny win and stop putting it off.  But the idea still makes me want to stay in my room.

The thought of everyone staring at your hearing aids and body is still really daunting, and it's understandable that you're worried about being the odd one out. It takes a lot of courage to even consider drawing when you're feeling so self-conscious. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're all alone with this. Maybe taking that tiny step, like drawing a quick line, could be a way to get a 'tiny win' and start to build some confidence. What would it mean to you to take that first step, even if it's just for yourself?

It takes a lot of courage to even consider drawing when you're feeling so self-conscious. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're all alone with this. Maybe taking that tiny step, like drawing a quick line, could be a way to get a 'tiny win' and start to build some confidence. What would it mean to you to take that first step, even if it's just for yourself?
